claim_text: HBM is central to AI workloads and demand for high-bandwidth memory continues to rise with AI training and inference growth.
notes: Micron explicitly ties HBM advanced packaging expansion to AI data-center demand.

claim_text: SK hynix had completed HBM supply discussions for the following year by late 2025 and said HBM4 shipments would begin in Q4 2025.
notes: Supports strong forward demand and early HBM4 ramp.

claim_text: SK hynix completed HBM4 development and prepared mass production in 2025.
notes: Supports frontier HBM supply race among a very small set of vendors.

claim_text: Micron began shipping 12-high 36GB HBM4 samples to major customers in June 2025.
notes: Official Micron release available in translated newsroom variants.

claim_text: Micron's new HBM advanced packaging facility in Singapore is scheduled to begin operations in 2026, with meaningful packaging-capacity expansion beginning in 2027.
notes: Strong evidence that packaging capacity itself needs major expansion.

claim_text: Samsung began mass production and commercial shipments of HBM4 in February 2026 and expects HBM sales to more than triple in 2026 versus 2025.
notes: Confirms both competitive HBM4 ramp and demand expectations.

claim_text: TSMC's 2024 annual report says strong AI demand boosted advanced packaging and notes CoWoS-L volume production started in 2024.
notes: Direct evidence of packaging demand and technology ramp.

claim_text: TSMC said strong AI demand fueled revenue growth in leading-edge logic and advanced packaging in 2024.
notes: Supports advanced packaging as a growth and capacity planning issue.

claim_text: The U.S. Department of Commerce said that a lack of 2.5D technology capacity had been a significant chokepoint in meeting rapidly increasing demand for generative AI products and services.
notes: This is the clearest official external statement that advanced packaging is a binding bottleneck.

claim_text: Advanced packaging is a critical supply-chain capability for AI and high-performance compute, which is why Commerce awarded Amkor funding for a U.S. advanced packaging facility.
notes: Supports packaging bottleneck and domestic resilience angle.

claim_text: Advanced substrates are foundational to future advanced packaging and are not currently produced in the U.S. at scale, motivating CHIPS investments in Absolics, Applied Materials, and ASU.
notes: Supports substrate/materials layer as a meaningful future constraint.

claim_text: The U.S. is treating advanced packaging as strategic enough to fund a national piloting and packaging facility through Natcast and NAPMP.
notes: Supports the view that packaging bottlenecks are structurally important.

claim_text: HBM alone is the only real bottleneck in AI accelerator scaling.
source_type: inference contradicted by official packaging evidence
notes: Official sources point to packaging as a major bottleneck too.

claim_text: Advanced substrate and packaging-material constraints are already the single dominant bottleneck today.
notes: Evidence supports strategic importance, but not yet that this is the single tightest point.
